description: This table tracks all campaign and marketing interactions at the subscriber level. It stores which bonus campaigns and promotional campaigns were sent to each subscriber, when they were sent, how many times, and whether they were delivered successfully or failed. It also tracks whether subscribers responded to campaigns or ignored them (non-responders). It contains campaign response codes, action keys for customer segments, and rolling time window aggregates over 2 days, 7 days, 30 days, 180 days, and 365 days. Use this table when the query involves campaign performance, campaign delivery, campaign response, marketing lifecycle, or any promotional activity tracking. This table does NOT contain revenue, usage, recharge, or subscriber profile information.
granularity: one record per subscriber with rolling aggregates
example_queries: subscribers who did not respond to any campaign in the last 30 days
how many promotions were delivered successfully last week
customers who received bonus campaigns but never responded
never_use_for: revenue, usage, recharge, demographics, device, plan, subscription products

description: This table stores the mapping between subscribers and their assigned audience segments for campaign targeting. Each record links a subscriber MSISDN to a specific segment ID and segment name. It also tracks the execution counter for how many times the segmentation has been run. Use this table when the query is about which campaign audience segment a subscriber belongs to, segment membership, or segment-based audience selection. This is NOT for value-based segmentation or ARPU bands.
granularity: one record per subscriber per segment
example_queries: which audience segment does this subscriber belong to
list all subscribers in segment X
how many times has segmentation been executed
never_use_for: revenue, usage, value segmentation, ARPU, demographics, recharge, campaign delivery status

description: This is the detailed recharge fact table for prepaid subscribers. It contains transaction-level recharge events enriched with subscriber-level rolling aggregates. It stores recharge amount, denomination, channel or type of recharge (e-wallet, retail, bank), recharge date and hour, recharge frequency metrics, rolling recharge revenue aggregates over 1 day, 15 days, 90 days, and 180 days, and average recharge metrics. Use this table when the query specifically asks about recharge behavior, recharge patterns, recharge amounts, recharge channels, top-up frequency, or recharge revenue trends. This table provides the deepest level of recharge analytics with denominations and channels.
granularity: transaction-level events with subscriber-level rolling aggregates
example_queries: average recharge amount in the last 90 days
subscribers who recharge through e-wallet channel
recharge frequency trend over 180 days
most common recharge denomination
never_use_for: voice usage, data usage, SMS, campaign tracking, demographics, plan info, real-time events

description: This table is strictly a ledger for ADD-ON product subscription events (activations and renewals). It tracks when a subscriber purchases extra products: data bundles, VAS (value-added services), roaming packs, and digital services. Each record represents a single add-on purchase event. Use this table ONLY when the query is about buying or renewing specific add-on products, bundles, or VAS.
granularity: one record per subscriber per add-on subscription event
example_queries: subscribers who subscribed to a data bundle via the app
how many VAS products does the subscriber have
renewal rate for roaming packs
revenue from a specific Unlimited Social product purchase event
never_use_for: base Account/Subscriber IDs, MSISDN, Activation Date, subscriber tenure, Cust Dealer, activation channels, Prepaid/Postpaid line types, demographics

description: This is the master CRM account and demographic profile table. It holds the core identity of the subscriber: Account IDs, Subscriber IDs, MSISDN Index, and base line type (Prepaid/Postpaid). It tracks the origins and age of the account: Activation Date, subscriber tenure (Age on Network), Cust Dealer, Activation Source/Channel, and onboarding method. It also stores human demographics, external credit ratings, device details, plan migrations, and static service flags (Mobile App User, TV service). Use this table for ANY query asking for Account/Subscriber IDs, activation dates/channels, tenure, dealers, base line types, or demographic profiles.
example_queries: numeric Subscriber ID, MSISDN, or Customer Account Number
Cust Dealer or Activation channel who activated the SIM
subscriber tenure, age on network, or activation date
current subscribed service category like Mobile Postpaid
is this customer a Mobile App User or have TV service
never_use_for: add-on product purchases (bundles/VAS), real-time lifecycle status changes (Active/Grace), loan amounts, dynamic usage metrics

description: This is the consolidated usage and revenue fact table used for segmentation, ARPU tracking, and overall subscriber spending. It provides the most granular breakdown of usage and aggregated revenue: data usage and revenue, voice usage split by on-net and off-net, IDD, SMS usage, and roaming. Critically, this table tracks overall 'Prepaid Subscription Revenue', 'Prepay Spending', and prepaid revenue breakdown by service type. Use this table when the query needs detailed usage breakdowns (like on-net vs off-net from bundles), monthly/rolling trend comparisons, ARPU calculations, GCC roaming data, or total prepaid subscription revenue/spending.
granularity: one record per subscriber per snapshot date
example_queries: total prepaid subscription revenue over a rolling 5-week period
prepaid subscription spending in the last week
on-net vs off-net voice usage from local bundle offers
ARPU trend and prepaid segment profitability
prepaid revenue breakdown by service type
never_use_for: real-time or instant metrics, digital wallet transactions, mobile money, fintech, Money Trans Amount, individual product activation events, campaign tracking

description: This table captures near real-time transactional events AND dynamic billing/lifecycle statuses. It stores voice/SMS/data session events (including the individual event-level revenue, charge, or cost for those specific OG/IDD calls and texts). It is the EXCLUSIVE table for digital wallet transactions, mobile money, fintech, and 'Money Trans Amount'. Crucially, it tracks all loan and credit activity (airtime advances, outstanding debt) and dynamic lifecycle status changes (Active/Grace). Use this table for real-time/instant events, wallet/mobile money transactions, event-level call/SMS charges, OR anytime a query asks for loan balances, debt, or current lifecycle state.
granularity: one record per subscriber per event or state change
example_queries: what is the customer's current lifecycle status (Active or Grace)
total numeric Money Trans Amount for this subscriber's wallet
mobile money and fintech service adoption
real-time segmentation of high-value users
event-level revenue generated from outgoing (OG) SMS or IDD calls
never_use_for: demographics, base plan migrations (use Profile), overall 30-day ARPU tracking, campaign delivery tracking
